channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Exclusive: Pence calls Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs pose a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walkback of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rumor of monkey trade for child leads to Missouri foster mom's arrest
item.description: Officials say new child abuse charges filed against a Winfield woman who has fostered children for a decade are "just the tip of the iceberg.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, KRON4

item.title: 1,700 pounds of butter recalled over bacteria contamination
item.description: Nearly a ton of salted butter is being recalled because, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, the product is "contaminated with elevated levels of coliform," a bacteria found in animals' digestive tracts and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KREX 5

item.title: Trump: Israel would be 'leader' of Iran strike if Tehran doesn't give up nuclear weapons program
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Israel would be the “leader” of a potential military strike against Iran if Tehran doesn’t give up its nuclear weapons program. Trump made the comments ahead of this weekend's scheduled talks involving U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dolphins player Tyreek Hill and his wife were involved in a domestic dispute, police say
item.description: SUNNY ISLES BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Miami Dolphins wide receiver Tyreek Hill and his wife were involved in a domestic dispute at their South Florida high-rise condominium, but no charges were filed, according to a police report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Interior Department staff, including those at national parks, offered buyouts and early retirement
item.description: Employees across the Department of the Interior have until the end of Wednesday to respond to the latest offer to take buyouts or early retirement, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press, as the Trump administration continues its efforts to reduce the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: She survived being shot in the head as teen. Now she's sharing her final message
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now in hospice.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp
item.description: If approved, the increase for a “forever” stamp and similar increases for postcards, metered letters and international mail would take effect July 13.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump replaces Patel with Army secretary as ATF director
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has been removed as the acting director of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration appeals court decision ordering AP journalists back into White House events
item.description: The Trump administration said Wednesday it would appeal a federal court decision that ordered it to re-admit Associated Press journalists to White House events on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Four Americans accused of Congo coup attempt now facing federal charges in Salt Lake City
item.description: Four Americans accused of participating in a failed coup attempt in the Democratic Republic of Congo will now go to trial in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo have been charged by the U.S. Justice Department with staging an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Are dire wolves de-extinct? Genetically engineered pups spark controversy
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LeBron James becomes first pro male athlete to have likeness depicted in Ken doll
item.description: LeBron James has become the first professional male athlete to have his likeness depicted in a Ken doll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Previous execution reveals how South Carolina will put 2nd inmate to death by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina is legally required to keep some protocols secret as it prepares to put a man to death by firing squad for the murder of a public safety officer, but the only previous execution by bullets the state carried out last month reveals a few key details.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Karen Read asks Supreme Court justice for emergency stay of trial with jury selection underway
item.description: Karen Read's legal team is asking for her second murder trial to be put on pause pending a petition to the Supreme Court arguing she is facing double jeopardy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 96-year-old resident at southern Utah care home dies after being found in pool
item.description: A resident of a senior living facility in southern Utah has died after being found in the community's pool on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: About $1.8 billion in federal money for Cornell and Northwestern is frozen, the White House says
item.description: Cornell University and Northwestern University say they haven't received any notice from the U.S. government about paused federal funding even though the White House says more than $1 billion for Cornell and about $790 million for Northwestern have been frozen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump botching tariff messaging because he lacks empathy: Cenk Uygur
item.description: "Why is [Trump] blowing it? Partly because he lacks empathy," Uygur told NewsNation's Leland Vittert on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shake Shack brings Dubai Chocolate Pistachio Shake to select L.A. locations
item.description: For those who were unsuccessful in recreating the viral Dubai chocolate bar or can’t afford a trip to get an authentic bar, Shake Shack has you covered. The fast-casual chain launched its Dubai Chocolate Pistachio Shake on Monday at select locations in New York City, Los Angeles, and Miami.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge tells government to provide evidence, or case against Columbia student Mahmoud Khalil is over
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge in Louisiana said she would terminate the case against Mahmoud Khalil if the government does not provide evidence this week justifying their attempted deportation of the Columbia University student activist.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break was executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was pronounced dead at 6:12 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fake job seekers using AI reportedly flooding job market
item.description: (KRON) -- Fake job seekers are utilizing artificial intelligence to try and fool recruiters conducting remote interviews, according to a report in CNBC.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, KRON4

item.title: The trial of an ex-deputy who shot an unarmed Black woman in her home is moved to another city
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The murder trial of a former Illinois sheriff's deputy who fatally shot Sonya Massey, an unarmed black woman, in her Springfield home last summer will be moved out of the capital city to Peoria because of intense publicity, a judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas college students reach across the aisle in an era of deep division
item.description: Both organizations have emerged against a backdrop of divisive politics and deep polarization throughout the country.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rejects new trials for 2 convicted of human smuggling in death of family of 4 from India
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday rejected requests for new trials for two men convicted on human smuggling charges in the deaths of four members of a family from India who froze to death while trying to cross the Canadian border into Minnesota during a blizzard in 2022. U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Last chance to claim $1,400 stimulus check from 2021 nears
item.description: Taxpayers who haven’t filed their 2021 tax returns may still be eligible to receive one (or more) stimulus payments sent during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, KRON4

item.title: Small plane skids off runway at Oregon airport, ends up in bay
item.description: A small private plane attempting to land at an Oregon airport skidded off the runway and into a body of water, leaving 5 people injured.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Foran, WGN 9

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Stocks sink globally as Trump pushes ahead with trade war
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much financial markets would love to see him do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
